title: pyrola incarnata subsp. dahurica
intro: Pyrola incarnata subsp. dahurica, also known as Dahurian Wintergreen, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, coughs, and digestive problems, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. Dahurian Wintergreen can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although its safety and efficacy should be further researched and validated.
